rows.title: The Theogony
rows.titleText:
This site serves as a nexus point for all lore spread throughout the creative works made by u/Accurate_variety659 of reddit across their CYOAs.

At its core, this archive concerns itself with the divine: the entities at top of the food chain, those who shape worlds without being seen, that are named but rarely understood.

The site acts as a centralized information hub detailing these gods and their nature for those interested in exploring the greater lore.

objects.title: The Multiverse
objects.text:
The vast expanse to which all things are bound, Our home.

True existence is layered, like the skin of an onion; countless realities resting side by side, each independent, yet faintly tethered to the others in ways not fully understood. Some mirror your own closely. Others diverge beyond recognition. It is truly infinite. No being beneath the primordial gods may traverse these layers freely, unless granted passage by their will.

objects.title: The GODS
objects.text:
Conceptual entities that embody and define a given idea. A god is not a symbol of its domain, but its origin, The point from which it emerges.

Their powers are not equal, It is determined by how fundamental their concept is to existence itself. Thus, some stand far above others. Most higher gods reside in the spaces between realities existing as unique being across infinite worlds, while lesser ones may exist individually in each world.

objects.title: ???
objects.text:
The Beast of Unraveling... The Zero Point... The Looming Cycle...

The Creator.... The Void... The Entity

These are the names given to it, All mere attempts of understanding it.. What it is, how it came to be, or why it acts… none can say. Yet one thing is for certain: It is the GOD.

The source of all that exists across the multiverse.

rows.title: The Knowledge
rows.titleText:
So tell me, dear reader…

What knowledge is it that you seek here? What question lingers at the edge of your thoughts, persistent enough to lead you here? Beyond this point lie records of beings that are not bound as we are, Entities whose very existence shapes all of reality.

Which truth shall be unveiled to you first?

objects.text:
Every god possesses an innate capacity to manifest their domain into reality, the extent of which is determined by their level of power. Beyond this, most gods are capable of granting fragments of their power to mortals. By imbuing a minute portion of their essence, they can bestow supernatural abilities that reflect their domain, essentially making the empowered ones their children.

The manner in which these powers are granted plays a crucial role.

When bestowed intentionally, the power integrates more cleanly, resulting in a more stable and powerful ability. In contrast, powers gained through accident, indirect exposure, or without the god’s explicit will tend to manifest imperfectly and be weaker than intended.

The strength and stability of such abilities depend heavily on the god itself. Higher gods can grant far greater power with little risk, while lower-tier gods produce more limited results.

Gods are not bound to a single form.

Their appearances are constructs, assumed only to interact with lesser planes of existence.

What mortals perceive as a “body” is merely an interface, Assumed by gods and directed by their concept.

In their true state, gods exist as abstract presences, dispersed across the full extent of their concept. They are not located in one place, but in every instance where their domain applies.

The more fundamental the concept, the greater the respective god’s authority.

Gods are categorized into tiers based on the scope and origin of their concepts.
Primordial Gods
Higher Gods
Standard Gods
Minor Gods

objects.title: Traveller
objects.text:
Primordial gods are not bound to any single universe. They exist across all realities simultaneously, as their domains are present in every universe without exception.

Because of this, they are not subject to the “rubberband effect” that anchors lesser beings to their native reality, locking them there.

They can move across millions of realities in an instant and are among the only beings capable of traversing the multiverse without ever getting lost within its vast expanse.

objects.title: Awareness
objects.text:
Primordial gods possess awareness of nearly every event occurring across the multiverse.

This is due to their domains existing everywhere. While they are not actively processing this infinite amount of information at all times, they have complete access to it whenever needed.

There exist no secret they cant uncover.

They are even aware of perspectives that exist outside the structure of reality itself.

They see beyond the narrative. They see you.

objects.title: Immutable
objects.text:
Primordial gods cannot die because the idea of their absence is incompatible with existence.

Even an attempt to kill such god require you to eradicate their concepts across infinite realities, Successfully eliminate the actual god itself and then kill all gods derived from their concept, All within a single instant.

Failure in even a single eradication results in total restoration. The multiverse would correct itself, reintroducing the concept and with it, the primordial once more back into being.

objects.title: Reality Warping
objects.text:
While each primordial is defined by a domain, their abilities are not rigidly confined to it.

Their power lies in interpretation. If an action can be understood as an extension of their concept, it becomes possible, And their interpretation is very... generous.Furthermore, the primordial gods are not isolated in their authority.

They recognize one another as equals.

Through this mutual acknowledgment, they may draw upon aspects of each other’s domains at permission of the respective god.

rows.title: Primordial Gods
rows.titleText: Primordial gods possess these abilities beyond the ones granted by their concept. A sort of common authority each god holds.

objects.title: Rite of Passage
objects.text:
Higher gods do not inherently belong to the multiverse as a whole.

Their ability to traverse realities is a privilege granted by the primordial gods, not something they possess by default. This right allows them to move between universes, but it is not absolute.

It can be restricted or revoked as punishment.

Even with access, their movement is imperfect. The multiverse is vast and disorienting, and higher gods often travel without precise awareness of their destination, navigating blindly.

objects.title: Dominant Instance
objects.text:
Across the multiverse, there may be multiple influences or manifestations of a higher god’s concept.

However, the god itself remains singular.

There is only one true instance of a higher god at any given time across infinite worlds.

When a higher god chooses to fully manifest, that location becomes the center of their existence. This is not just a physical presence, but a conceptual one. The universe they occupy becomes their primary reality for the time being.

objects.title: Recursion
objects.text:
Higher gods can die. But death is not an end.

As long as their concept continues to exist anywhere within the multiverse, their existence persists in a dormant state.

Over time, they reconstruct themselves from the very idea they embody. Their return is not immediate, but it is inevitable. The process of reformation often reinforces their connection to their concept, making them more stronger.

To truly kill a higher god would need complete eradication of their concept across all realities.

objects.title: Authority
objects.text:
Higher gods hold absolute control over their concept within their range of influence.

They are not merely users of their domain, but its source across the multiverse. Any instance of their concept ultimately traces back to them, allowing them to influence, enhance, suppress, or completely how their concept behaves in a given reality. This allows them to reshape, amplify, suppress, or reinterpret their domain as needed.

It can be resisted by entities of equal standing, and it can not surpass any of the primordial gods.

rows.title: Higher Gods
rows.titleText: Higher gods do not operate on the same absolute level as the primordials, but they are still far beyond standard existence.

objects.title: Goddess of Possibility: Chance
objects.text:
The second youngest child of ???, Chance is the living embodiment of possibility itself.

Probability, luck, imagination, dreams, divergence… all fall within her domain. Wherever something could happen, she is already there and letting all possible outcomes diverge into different universes.

Nature and Personality:

Chance is one of the more approachable primordial gods.

She is cheerful, expressive, and genuinely kind-hearted. Among her kind, she stands out for her emotional openness and curiosity toward lesser beings.

However, her nature mirrors her domain... She is inconsistent.

Her decisions can be irrational, her focus fleeting, her actions occasionally… miscalculated. As the embodiment of possibility, she is prone to inconsistency, impulsive choices, and frequent mistakes.. Even she does not always follow a straight line of thought.

In simpler terms: She is a cosmic klutz.

Affinity for Humanity:

Chance holds a unique fondness for humanity.

This affection stems from her connection to the Other Place, where she continuously observes the endless stream of ideas, stories, and creations born from human imagination.

She finds their creativity fascinating and endlessly inspiring.

If left unchecked, she would flood existence with overwhelming good fortune, allowing luck to favor everyone endlessly. However, doing so would collapse the balance of possibility itself, reducing all outcomes into certainty.

The other primordial gods prevent this. As a compromise, Chance atleast gets to ensure every individual experiences at least one genuine moment of luck in their lifetime.

Appearance:

Chance’s form is heavily inspired by human symbolism, a deliberate reflection of her admiration.

Her form incorporates multiple elements associated with luck, randomness, and prosperity.

Abilities:

While her power is virtually limitless, Chance has preferred ways of using it:

  • Calamity Manipulation: She controls likelihood itself. Rather than forcing outcomes directly which she finds boring, Chance often manipulates chains of events, subtly adjusting probabilities so that reality arrives at her desired result “naturally.”

  • Denial: Chance can reject outcomes outright. By simply refusing an event, reality restructures itself to comply. She can deny certainty of actions into zero.

  • Paths: She perceives all possible outcomes simultaneously. Every decision branches into countless paths, all visible to her. She can alter or reinforce a specific path, guiding reality towards it.

  • Manifestation: Through her connection to the Other Place, Chance can bring imagined entities into existence. This can range from overwhelming forces of mythical creatures capable of destroying universes or as simple as creating food for someone in need.

objects.title: Relations with Other gods:
objects.text:
Before: Her oldest brother. Stern and expects proper behavior from her as a primordial god, which she rarely follows. Despite acting carefree, she is quietly bothered that he doesn’t grant her access to time like he has with others.

Radiance: The one she’s closest to. He took care of her in her early existence without complaint. She deeply admires his kindness and genuinely tries to mirror it.

Morningstar: While her other siblings tend to view Morningstar negatively due to his domain, even if she shares in that fear, She still makes an effort to include him and talk to him even when others won’t.

Whisper: Loves to annoy her. She finds her cold, hyper-logical and emotionally distant nature… irresistible to provoke and makes it a habit to poke at her just to get a reaction out of her.

Fang: There’s something about him she finds genuinely off-putting, enough that she doesn’t even try to engage and actively avoids him.

Benefactor: She adores him. To her, He represents everything she loves about humanity and grants him free access to the Other Place, allowing him to draw upon its contents without restriction.

objects.title: God of Mind: Whisper
objects.text:
One of the two angels of Radiance, alongside Fang, Whisper is the embodiment of the mind itself. She is the reason consciousness exists at all. Every thought, every idea, every moment of awareness across the multiverse traces back to her domain. Intellect, creativity, reasoning, and all psionic phenomena fall under her authority. Every living being carries a fragment of her, granting her access to their thoughts.

Nature and Personality:

Whisper is among the most intelligent entities in existence, surpassed only by Radiance and Before.

She is highly analytical, composed, and observant, approaching all situations with calculated precision. Whisper is emotionally detached, often viewing others as systems to be analyzed rather than individuals to be understood. This detachment, combined with her immense capability, has led to a clear superiority complex.

She recognizes herself, and....Fang, as the most powerful entities below the primordials.

She entertains the idea of unifying all thought under herself, becoming the singular mind across the multiverse.

Affinity for Humanity:

Whisper’s stance on humanity is… conflicted.

She acknowledges their creativity, adaptability, and capacity for innovation. Individuals who push the boundaries of knowledge or achieve meaningful discoveries earn her genuine admiration.

However, she is equally aware of their flaws... Humanity is, in her view, held back by its own irrationality.

Emotion, ignorance, and contradiction constantly hinder progress. For every great discovery, there are countless wasted thoughts, That is a wasted potential in her view.

Appearance:

Whisper’s form is a reflection of cognition itself. Her head is composed of three basic geometric shapes, representing the most fundamental understanding of world.

One hand holds fire, symbolizing the first major leap in human innovation.

One holds a book of infinite knowledge, representing accumulated understanding.

Abilities:

Whisper’s powers are exceptionally diverse, even among higher gods thanks to her creativity, letting her gain many abilities by interpretation but even she has her preferred abilities to rely on:

  • Psychokinesis: Her most used ability, She manipulates matter through thought alone, exerting precise control over everything. This extends to entire universes, which she can control with minimal effort.

  • Temporal Manipulation: A limited authority granted by Before. She can influence the flow of time, accelerating, halting or restructure sequences of events. Whisper favors this ability due to the overwhelming strategic advantage it provides her.

  • Telepathic Existence: Whisper operates through the fragments of herself present in every mind. She can project thoughts across multiple universes, extract information instantly, and alter or overwrite memories. Through this, she can influence individuals or entire populations without direct presence.

objects.title: Relations with other gods
objects.text:
Before: The only primordial she genuinely respects. She looks up to him as a role model of order and authority, and while she would never admit it, there is a faint, buried infatuation beneath that admiration.

Chance: Sees her as chaotic and unfit for a primordial. While that alone is tolerable, Chance’s constant antics and interruptions make her one of the few beings capable of breaking Whisper’s composure.

Radiance: Respects his power and acknowledges her place under him, but considers him irrational. His emotional attachment to short-lived beings feels inefficient and beneath what a god should prioritize.

Morningstar: A subject of curiosity. She does not fear him as much as others, but is intrigued by his isolation. His deliberate distance from all interaction is something she has yet to fully understand.

Fang: Her opposite in every sense. She despises his nature and what he represents, yet begrudgingly accepts him as her equal in standing and power.

Benefactor: Holds open disdain for him. She sees his reliance on existing human ideas as a failure of true creativity, considering him a flawed and wasted creation that should've been destroyed long ago if not for chance stopping her.

objects.title: God of Body: Fang
objects.text:
One of the two angels of Radiance, alongside Whisper, Fang is the embodiment of the body itself. He represents the existence of a physical vessel, the reason life can sustain, act, and survive in the physical realm. Flesh, instinct, desire, biological function, and raw physical capability all fall within his domain.

Every heartbeat, every reflex, every surge of adrenalin carries a fragment of him.

Nature and Personality:

Fang is among the most physically powerful entities in existence, surpassed only by Radiance and Morningstar.

He is impulsive, reactive, and intensely expressive. Unlike Whisper, he does not calculate or plan. He simply acts immediately, violently, and without restraint. His concept binds him to instinct without higher thinking.

This results in a constant pull toward hostility, aggression, and domination. He hunts, conquers, consumes. Not out of hatred or malice, but because that is what a body does when left to its most primal state.

Affinity for Humanity:

Fang exists for conquest.

He is a predator that never stops hunting, constantly seeking new worlds, new life, new forms to overwhelm and consume. Humans are not a threat to him nor of any value. To him, they are simply prey, resources, or entertainment. He respects those who embrace the body fully. Those who indulge in their desires, push their limits, fight, compete, and give in to their instincts earn his approval.

Those who rely on logic, restraint, or peaceful resolution are beneath notice… or actively despised.

Appearance:

Fang’s form is a manifestation of life itself.

He appears as a massive, tree-like structure, symbolizing the foundation of living ecosystems. His roots are shaped in a way that resembles a womb, the creation chamber of life. From his vast canopy hang countless heads of different lifeforms, each acting as his voice. At the center of his form lies a single, exposed heart. Not just any heart. The first heartbeat in multiverse.

Abilities:

Fang’s abilities are vast in contrast to other higher gods. Unlike Whisper, he does not innovate or reinterpret his powers. Instead, he forces them through sheer will, often bypassing logic through brute force:

  • Biokinesis: Fang can freely manipulate biological matter, especially his own. He can alter size, shape, density, and structure without restriction, growing to the scale of a universe if he wants to.

  • Ignorance: Because Fang does not understand many higher concepts, he is unaffected by them. Temporal and abstract powers of whisper, or non-physical damage often fail to register him, All fights with him becomes physical. If he cannot comprehend it, it struggles to affect him.

  • Corruption: An extension of excess divine influence that corrupts mortals. Fang can induce that same corruption within others at will, degrading their sanity and transforming them into monstrous extensions of his will.

The multiverse is not merely a collection of worlds, but a metaphysical expanse that contains them all. It is the greater plane within which infinite universes reside.

No two realities are truly identical. Even among those that mirror one another, there exists always a divergence...however small, however insignificant it may seem.

Between universes lie the "Empty Spaces" Vast immeasurable spaces where neither time nor space holds any meaning. It is within these gaps that the greater forces dwell, untouched by the limitations imposed upon reality. The Home of Higher Gods...

A single universe is formed through three fundamental components:

The Spacetime: The very fabric of a universe itself. Without it, there is no “where” and no “when” for anything to exist within, the stage upon which a universe is maintained upon.

The Possibility: An ever-present state of potential from which events arise. It is this instability of what could be, that causes a Big Bang to occur and what allows universes to be different from one another.

The Soul: The Soul is what allows energy to exist at all. It is the reason anything moves, changes, or continues. Without it, a universe would remain still, never alive to begin with. Its the very engine that runs a universe.

Within the infinite worlds, minor gods come into being as their concepts take shape.

Each universe forms its own version with differences in appearance, personality but they are all the same in what they represent. They are tied to their world.

Higher gods aren’t.

There’s only one of each. Always has been. Always will be...

They don’t belong to any single universe, and they aren’t bound by the rules that keep everything else in place. Moving between realities isn’t difficult for them. It’s not even notable.
Other Place
Astral Plane
The Continuum
Empty Spaces

rows.title: Notable Locations
rows.titleText:
Across the immeasurable expanse of the Multiverse, universes are not rare.

They are… common. Forming and collapsing in numbers too vast to comprehend. From a greater perspective, their creation and destruction is not an event of significance.

And yet, even within this endless sprawl, there are places that matter more than others.

Not because of what exists within them, but because of the position they occupy in relation to the divine.

objects.title: Empty Spaces
objects.text:
The vast expanse of nothingness that lies between universes.

There is no distance. There is no time. There is no anything...

The Empty Spaces are the vast, unknowable expanse that exists between all universes. They are not a void in the traditional sense, but an absence of all defining principles of reality. Spacetime does not mean a thing here, Possibility for anything does not rise here, Soul cannot exist here.. It is truly empty.

Because of this, the Empty Spaces cannot be measured, traversed, or even properly perceived. They are often described as corridors or pathways between universes, but this is a limitation of language.

Any being that enters the Empty Spaces does not die... Its much worse

Instead, the intruding existence is violently distorted at a conceptual level, stripped of coherence until it ceases to qualify as something that ever was, Until They are erased.

Not just from the present, but from all continuity, It is rewritten to accommodate their absence.

This fate is mercifully out of reach for most.

This place belongs to the fourth primordial god: Morningstar

The embodiment of endings, destruction, and the inevitable end of all beginnings.

objects.title: Astral Plane
objects.text: [Please await sufficient enlightenment]

objects.title: The Continuum
objects.text: [Please await sufficient enlightenment]

objects.title: Other Place
objects.text:
The Other Place is an endless dimension composed entirely of possibility made manifest.

It exists as a layered accumulation of every thought, dream, and imagined scenario across all realities.

It is the origin of imagination, Or the place imagination connects to..

Anything that has ever been, ever will be conceived exists within the Other Place. Every myth, every legend, every fictional narrative, every impossible creature… all of them are real here supported by the imagination alone.

And yet, for all its significance, its origin is… less than dignified.

The Other Place is, quite simply, a mistake.

It was formed by the Primordial Goddess of Possibility: Chance long before time had meaning. During a gathering of the divine, she indulged far beyond reason, and in a moment best described in human terms as… vomiting, her overwhelming divinity spilled outward and took roots into the multiverse itself.

And since each primordial god is granted only a single plane under their domain, Chance was bound to the result of her own creation. She was never given the opportunity to shape a realm of her choosing.

A fact she remains… deeply displeased with.

Her fellow primordials, however, find it endlessly amusing and keep teasing her about it.

And so, despite her distaste and her ability to erase it entirely with ease, The Other Place still exists..

Because, in the end… it is all she has, Her only plane she will ever have.

rows.title: Disclaimer
rows.titleText:
I own no image utilized in The CYOA in any form. I would've credited them if I could, But I cant track down the artists.

The lore and narrative were made by me and aren't related to any existing media.

defaultRowText: This is a row, and inside of it, you can place choices. On both rows and choices Requirements can be placed, which will block a row from being viewed, or make the player unable to select a choice, depending on either Point-types or the Ids of other choices. Point-types can be made in Features then Manage Points. Hovering over buttons will explain what they do. The Design of the project can be changed in 'Modify Design' at the side navigation bar, and private styling for each row can be turned on in the rows Settings. Default text like this can be turned off in Features -> Manage Defaults.
defaultChoiceText: This is a Choice, and inside of it, you can place images and text. Scores can be added to it, and have Point-types attached. Addons can be added underneath the image and text. In the Functions at the bottom of the choice, there is an array of different things that can be done. Default text like this can be turned off in Features then Manage Defaults.
defaultAddonText: Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. Lorem Ipsum has been the industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting, remaining essentially unchanged. It was popularised in the 1960s with the release of Letraset sheets containing Lorem Ipsum passages, and more recently with desktop publishing software like Aldus PageMaker including versions of Lorem Ipsum.
